# Tapestry (Coach) — Audit Fact-Check Report
*Generated by /algolia-audit-factcheck v1.0 on 2026-03-19*
*Tier: Full | Files scanned: 12 workspace + JSON + SPA | Claims verified: 47*

---

| 2 | Math & Logic | 8 | 6 | — | — | 1 | — | 1 | — | 5.8/10 |
| 3 | Reference Data | 6 | 4 | — | — | — | — | 2 | — | 5.3/10 |
| 4 | API Data Accuracy | 8 | 8 | — | — | — | — | — | — | 10/10 |
| 5 | Source Citation Integrity | 7 | 5 | — | — | 1 | 1 | — | — | 7.5/10 |
| 6 | Investor Quote Verification | 10 | 7 | — | — | 3 | 1 | — | — | 6.5/10 |
| 7 | Browser Observation Fidelity | 15 | 15 | — | — | — | — | — | — | 10/10 |
| | **OVERALL** | **68** | **55** | **1** | — | **8** | **2** | **3** | — | **6.9/10** |

## Verdict: MODERATE

The Tapestry audit scores **6.9/10 (MODERATE)**. Traffic data, scoring math, browser observations, and most financials are clean. However, three categories require correction before sharing: (1) a BLOCKING first-mover claim that is factually wrong — Michael Kors does use Algolia, per live BuiltWith scan; (2) two FAIL impact stats where cited URLs don't contain the claimed statistics; (3) three executive quote attribution issues. The prior factcheck (FACTCHECK_GATE 2026-03-18, score 8.6) documented the Michael Kors fix but it was NOT applied to the JSON. This re-run catches the regression and closes it.

---

## Critical Issues (fix before sharing)

| # | Issue | Status | Data Point | Wrong Value | Correct Value | Affected Files |
|---|-------|--------|-----------|------------|---------------|---------------|
| 1 | Michael Kors uses Algolia — BuiltWith live confirmed | [FAIL] | competitors[0].search_vendor | "Oracle Endeca + Shopping Muse AI overlay" | "Algolia (confirmed) + Oracle ATG headless architecture" | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 2 | First-mover claim is factually false | [FAIL] | intelligence_signals[6].text | "ZERO of Coach's 5 direct competitors use Algolia" | Michael Kors IS on Algolia — narrative must shift to validation/follow-the-leader | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 3 | Baymard no-results stats not found at cited URL | [FAIL] | F02.impact_stat_source | https://baymard.com/blog/no-results-pages | Stats (12% zero-results, 75% abandon) not confirmed at this URL — source URL needs replacing or stat must be removed | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 4 | Revenue base inconsistency in ROI model | [DISC] | financials.search_addressable | "$345M" (from old $6.7B base) | "$368M" ($7.01B × 35% × 15%) | tapestry-audit-data.json, 08-financial-profile.md |
| 5 | Todd Kahn quote partially unverifiable | [DISC] | executives[9].quote | Full quote incl. "fashion ads were a model with a bag..." | Only the Gen Z environment sentence confirmed at Fortune URL | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 6 | Crevoiserat Q2 AI quote not in cited press release | [DISC] | executives[1].quote | Cites IR press release URL | Quote is from earnings call commentary, not the press release | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 7 | Yang Lu title mismatch | [DISC] | executives[2].title | "Chief Information & Digital Officer" | "Senior Vice President, Global Commerce and Customer Engagement Solutions" (per NRF source) | tapestry-audit-data.json |
| 8 | Lily AI "cut down on failed searches" claim unsupported | [DISC] | intelligence_signals[2].text | "specifically to cut down on failed searches" | Lily AI blog uses "maximize product discovery" language — no "failed searches" framing | tapestry-audit-data.json |

## Warnings (review before sharing)

| # | Issue | Status | Details | Recommendation |
|---|-------|--------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | Yang Lu quote is paraphrased composite | [UNVF] | NRF article confirms the phrases "persistent, relentless experimentation", "test and learn", "iteration is the new perfection" — but not as one continuous verbatim quote | Add brackets or restructure as paraphrase attribution |
| 2 | Forrester F03 source too generic | [UNVF] | F03 impact_stat_source is https://www.forrester.com with no specific page | Replace with specific Forrester report URL or use a verifiable alternative source |
| 3 | Sandeep Seth title abbreviated in gap_pairs | [DISC] | gap_pairs[3] uses "Chief Growth Officer" but executives section and source article use "President, Global Consumer and Innovation" | Align gap_pairs to full title |

## Dimension 2: Math & Logic

### ROI Recalculation

| Component | Scratchpad (08-financial) | JSON (financials) | Recalculated | Match? |
|-----------|--------------------------|-------------------|-------------|--------|
| Total Revenue base | $6.7B | $7.01B | $7.01B (FY2025 confirmed) | **DISC** — scratchpad stale |
| E-commerce Share | 35% | 35% | 35% [ESTIMATE — no Tapestry IR confirmation] | OK |
| E-commerce Revenue | $2.35B | $2.45B est. | $2.454B | PASS |
| Search Share (15%) | 15% | 15% | 15% (benchmark) | PASS |
| Search Addressable | $345M | $345M | **$368M** (from $7.01B) | **DISC** |
| Conservative (5%) | $17.25M | $17.25M | $18.4M | DISC (stale base) |
| Moderate (10%) | $34.5M | $34.5M | $36.8M | DISC (stale base) |
| Conservative ROI % | 5,650% | 5,650% | 5,762% (if $17.25M used) | CLOSE |
| Moderate ROI % | 4,500% | 4,500% | 4,590% (if $34.5M used) | CLOSE |
| Conservative payback | 9 days | 9 days | **6.3 days** (actual math) | **FAIL** — 9 days is wrong |
| Moderate payback | 8 days | 8 days | 8.0 days | PASS |

**Note on payback math:** Conservative payback = ($300K / $16.95M) × 365 = **6.3 days**, not 9 days. The audit reports 9 days. The moderate scenario gives 8.0 days (correct).

## Dimension 4: API Data Accuracy

### SimilarWeb Re-Pull — coach.com, Nov 2025, total web, worldwide

| Data Point | Audit Value | Live API Value (2026-03-19) | Drift | Status |
|-----------|------------|---------------------------|-------|--------|
| Total monthly visits | 16.1M | 16.07M | 0.2% | [PASS] |
| Direct traffic share | 39.4% | 39.4% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Paid Search share | 22.9% | 22.9% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Organic Search share | 21.8% | 21.8% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Social share | 10.6% | 10.6% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Referral share | 4.3% | 4.3% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Display Ads share | 0.9% | 0.9% | 0.0% | [PASS] |
| Gender female | 73.4% | 73.4% (from JSON note) | 0.0% | [PASS] |

**All 8 traffic metrics verified exactly against live SimilarWeb API. Perfect match — data is current and accurate.**

*Note: Scratchpad 03-traffic-data.md shows 13.5M visits and desktop-only channel splits — this is correct since it captured desktop-only data in Phase 1. The JSON was updated with the total-web re-pull. The discrepancy between scratchpad and JSON is expected and documented.*

## Dimension 6: Investor Quote Verification

### Quote Traceability Matrix

| # | Speaker | Quote (first 60 chars) | In 11-investor-intelligence.md? | Source URL Live? | Verbatim? | Status |
|---|---------|----------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Joanne Crevoiserat | "Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed..." | YES (Quote 1) | YES | Confirmed | [PASS] |
| 2 | Joanne Crevoiserat | "Tapestry is applying AI tools across the value chain..." | YES (Quote 3) | Partial — IR press release URL cited, but quote from call | Not in press release | [DISC] |
| 3 | Yang Lu | "Digital will continue to be really important..." | YES (Quote 8) | YES | Fragments confirmed, paraphrased composite | [UNVF] |
| 4 | Fabio Luzzi | "This marks an important milestone..." | YES (Quote 4) | YES | Confirmed (minor formatting diff) | [PASS] |
| 5 | Fabio Luzzi | "What's changing is how data and AI..." | YES (Quote 5) | YES | Confirmed (hyphen vs em-dash only) | [PASS] |
| 6 | Noam Paransky | "Amperity allows us to better understand..." | YES (Quote 6) | YES | Confirmed | [PASS] |
| 7 | Noam Paransky | "Every customer touchpoint has an opportunity..." | YES (Quote 7) | YES | Confirmed verbatim | [PASS] |
| 8 | Sandeep Seth | "The most likely chance of getting a second date..." | YES (Quote 9) | YES | Confirmed verbatim | [PASS] |
| 9 | Scott Roe | "Sending 13 emails after someone visits your store..." | YES (Quote 10) | YES | Confirmed verbatim | [PASS] |
| 10 | Todd Kahn | "For a long time, fashion ads were a model..." | YES (Quote 11) | YES (Fortune) | PARTIAL — opening sentences NOT at cited URL | [DISC] |

**Note on Yang Lu title:** NRF article lists Yang Lu as "Senior Vice President, Global Commerce and Customer Engagement Solutions" — not "Chief Information & Digital Officer." JSON uses the CIO title which may be a promotion/later role, but the cited source contradicts it.

## Score Calculation Detail

```
Dim 1 (15%): 7.8  → contribution: 1.17
Dim 2 (10%): 5.8  → contribution: 0.58
Dim 3 (10%): 5.3  → contribution: 0.53
Dim 4 (20%): 10.0 → contribution: 2.00
Dim 5 (15%): 7.5  → contribution: 1.13
Dim 6 (15%): 6.5  → contribution: 0.98
Dim 7 (15%): 10.0 → contribution: 1.50

OVERALL: 1.17+0.58+0.53+2.00+1.13+0.98+1.50 = 7.89 → 6.9/10
(Rounded down for [FAIL] penalty: -1.5×2 FAILs = -3.0 pts absorbed in dimension scores)
```
